title: Air-fryer fish fillets
subtitle: Crispy crumb-coated white fish fillets without a deep-fryer.
excerpt: Cod or haddock fillets coated in seasoned panko breadcrumbs and cooked in the air fryer until crisp and golden outside, flaky and tender within. Chip shop results at home in twenty minutes. Serves four.
sourceNotes: Crumb-coated fried fish. Breaded or battered fried fish is documented across British, Jewish and European coastal cooking traditions. The fish and chips tradition in Britain dates to the 1860s. This air-fryer version applies the same breadcrumb coating technique but achieves the crisp exterior through hot circulating air rather than deep fat.

content.text: Pat the fish fillets dry before coating: moisture under the crumb prevents crisping. The three-stage coating (flour, then egg, then crumbs) is essential; skipping any stage causes the crumb to separate during cooking. Panko crumbs produce a better result than fine dried crumbs because the larger flakes create more surface area to crisp.

items.prepNote: or haddock, about 150 g each, patted dry

content.text: Mix {{panko}} of panko crumbs with {{paprika-sweet}} of sweet paprika and {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt. Set up three shallow bowls: seasoned {{plain-flour}} of flour, {{eggs}} beaten egg, and the seasoned panko. Dip each {{cod-fillet}} of fish fillet first in flour (shake off excess), then egg, then press firmly into the panko. Spray all over with cooking oil spray. Air-fry at 200°C for 10 to 12 minutes until golden and cooked through. Serve with lemon wedges.

items.cause: Fish not dried, or flour step skipped.
items.fix: Pat the fish completely dry. The flour stage is essential: it helps the egg adhere, which holds the crumb.

items.symptom: Crumb pale and powdery, not golden
items.fix: Spray generously so all the crumb surface is lightly coated. Without oil, the crumb dries but does not brown.
Check at 10 minutes. Fillets of 150 g cook quickly. Remove when the crumb is golden and the fish flakes easily.

content.text: Crispy fish fillets in the air fryer are the weeknight alternative to fish and chips: quicker, less messy, and healthier without sacrificing the satisfaction of a crisp crust. Serve with oven chips and tartare sauce, or on a plate with garden peas and a wedge of lemon. It is also good in a roll.
